userdoc.notice: Library for converting numbers into strings and other string operations.

devdoc.author: Solady (https://github.com/vectorized/solady/blob/main/src/utils/LibString.sol)Modified from Solmate (https://github.com/transmissions11/solmate/blob/main/src/utils/LibString.sol) Note: For performance and bytecode compactness, most of the string operations are restricted to byte strings (7-bit ASCII), except where otherwise specified. Usage of byte string operations on charsets with runes spanning two or more bytes can lead to undefined behavior.

HexLengthInsufficient().details: The length of the output is too small to contain all the hex digits.

TooBigForSmallString().details: The length of the string is more than 32 bytes.

documentation.text: @dev The length of the output is too small to contain all the hex digits.
@dev The length of the string is more than 32 bytes.
@dev The constant returned when the `search` is not found in the string.
@dev Returns the base 10 decimal representation of `value`.
@dev Returns the base 10 decimal representation of `value`.

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`,
 left-padded to an input length of `length` bytes.
 The output is prefixed with "0x" encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte,
 giving a total length of `length * 2 + 2` bytes.
 Reverts if `length` is too small for the output to contain all the digits.

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`,
 left-padded to an input length of `length` bytes.
 The output is prefixed with "0x" encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte,
 giving a total length of `length * 2` bytes.
 Reverts if `length` is too small for the output to contain all the digits.

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`.
 The output is prefixed with "0x" and encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte.
 As address are 20 bytes long, the output will left-padded to have
 a length of `20 * 2 + 2` bytes.

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`.
 The output is prefixed with "0x".
 The output excludes leading "0" from the `toHexString` output.
 `0x00: "0x0", 0x01: "0x1", 0x12: "0x12", 0x123: "0x123"`.

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`.
 The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte.
 As address are 20 bytes long, the output will left-padded to have
 a length of `20 * 2` bytes.

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`.
 The output is prefixed with "0x", encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte,
 and the alphabets are capitalized conditionally according to
 https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-55

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`.
 The output is prefixed with "0x" and encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte.

@dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`.
 The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte.

@dev Returns the hex encoded string from the raw bytes.
 The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte.

@dev Returns the hex encoded string from the raw bytes.
 The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte.
@dev Returns the number of UTF characters in the string.

@dev Returns if this string is a 7-bit ASCII string.
 (i.e. all characters codes are in [0..127])
@dev Returns `subject` all occurrences of `search` replaced with `replacement`.
@dev Returns true if `search` is found in `subject`, false otherwise.
@dev Returns whether `subject` starts with `search`.
@dev Returns whether `subject` ends with `search`.
@dev Returns `subject` repeated `times`.

@dev Returns a copy of `subject` sliced from `start` to `end` (exclusive).
 `start` and `end` are byte offsets.

@dev Returns a copy of `subject` sliced from `start` to the end of the string.
 `start` is a byte offset.

@dev Returns all the indices of `search` in `subject`.
 The indices are byte offsets.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (string memory,string memory) pure returns (uint256[] memory)

documentation.text: @dev Returns a arrays of strings based on the `delimiter` inside of the `subject` string.

@dev Returns a concatenated string of `a` and `b`.
 Cheaper than `string.concat()` and does not de-align the free memory pointer.

@dev Returns a copy of the string in either lowercase or UPPERCASE.
 WARNING! This function is only compatible with 7-bit ASCII strings.

@dev Returns a string from a small bytes32 string.
 `s` must be null-terminated, or behavior will be undefined.
@dev Returns the small string, with all bytes after the first null byte zeroized.
@dev Returns the string as a normalized null-terminated small string.

@dev Returns a lowercased copy of the string.
 WARNING! This function is only compatible with 7-bit ASCII strings.

@dev Returns an UPPERCASED copy of the string.
 WARNING! This function is only compatible with 7-bit ASCII strings.
@dev Escapes the string to be used within HTML tags.

@dev Escapes the string to be used within double-quotes in a JSON.
 If `addDoubleQuotes` is true, the result will be enclosed in double-quotes.
@dev Escapes the string to be used within double-quotes in a JSON.
@dev Returns whether `a` equals `b`.
@dev Returns whether `a` equals `b`, where `b` is a null-terminated small string.

@dev Packs a single string with its length into a single word.
 Returns `bytes32(0)` if the length is zero or greater than 31.

@dev Packs two strings with their lengths into a single word.
 Returns `bytes32(0)` if combined length is zero or greater than 30.
@dev Directly returns `a` without copying.
